title: dactylorhiza majalis subsp. russowii
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis subsp. russowii, also known as the Russow's marsh orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its pinkish-purple flowers and distinctive growth habit. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the cardiovascular system and improve circulation. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza majalis subsp. russowii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of dried roots or extracts, and can be consumed as a tea or supplement to support overall health and well-being.
